description: The first run of a post-bl-8a9a bl in any pre-existing repo migrates in place: state_repo::ensure adopts the legacy .balls/worktree's balls/tasks into a new .balls/state-repo, then retire_legacy_worktree removes .balls/worktree AND deletes the project git's local balls/tasks branch, and absorb_plugins_dir moves any real .balls/plugins/*.json into the state checkout. For the dogfood bare repo this is high-stakes and irreversible-ish. Before anyone runs make install, verify on a throwaway clone of the bare repo: (a) the new .balls/state-repo carries the full task history, not just HEAD; (b) origin (GitHub origin/balls/tasks) stays the durable tracker and bl sync still round-trips after migration; (c) concurrent clones still on an old bl, and the shared origin, are not stranded by the local branch deletion; (d) the .balls/plugins/{github,github-issues}.json absorb-onto-tracker-branch is correct and the resulting main-branch deletion drift is expected and gets committed. This is the riskiest behaviour bl-8a9a shipped — confirm it on a copy before it touches the live repo.
title: Verify the bare-hub in-place migration is safe for the dogfooded /home/mark/dev/balls
